back_translation: When Jehoshaphat king of Judah returned safely to his palace in Jerusalem,
Jehu son of Hanani the seer went out to meet him and said to King Jehoshaphat, “Should you help the wicked and love those who hate the LORD? Because of this, wrath from the LORD is upon you.
Nevertheless, there is good found in you, for you have removed the Asherah poles from the land and have set your heart to seek God.”
Jehoshaphat dwelt in Jerusalem, and he went out again among the people from Beersheba to the hill country of Ephraim and brought them back to the LORD, the God of their fathers.
He appointed judges in the land, in all the fortified cities of Judah, city by city.
He said to the judges, “Consider carefully what you do, for you do not judge for man but for the LORD, who is with you when you give judgment.
Now therefore, let the fear of the LORD be upon you; be careful what you do, for with the LORD our God there is no injustice, no partiality, and no taking of bribes.”
Moreover, in Jerusalem Jehoshaphat appointed some of the Levites, priests, and heads of the families of Israel to judge for the LORD and to settle disputes; and they returned to Jerusalem.
He charged them, saying, “You shall act in the fear of the LORD, faithfully and with a whole heart.

Whatever case comes to you from your brothers who live in their cities—whether of bloodshed or of law, commandment, statutes, or ordinances—you shall warn them, so that they do not become guilty before the LORD and wrath come upon you and your brothers. Do this, and you will not become guilty.

Behold, Amariah the chief priest is over you in all matters of the LORD, and Zebadiah son of Ishmael, the leader of the house of Judah, in all the king's matters; and the Levites will serve as officers before you. Be strong and do it, and may the LORD be with the good.”
